description_long: This sculpture depicts a dramatic scene featuring a central muscular male figure, likely Laocoön, entangled by serpents along with two smaller male figures, presumably his sons. The figures are rendered in a highly dynamic and expressive manner, with twisting bodies and outstretched arms, conveying intense emotion and struggle. The serpents coil around the figures, emphasizing the sense of movement and tension. The sculpture is mounted on a rectangular plinth, which provides a stable base and elevates the composition. The surface appears dark and metallic, suggesting it is cast in bronze or a similar material. The overall craftsmanship is detailed, with careful attention to anatomical features, hair, and drapery.
description: A dramatic bronze sculpture of three entwined human figures and serpents, mounted on a rectangular plinth. The scene is highly expressive, with detailed anatomy and dynamic movement.
description_view_0: The front view shows the central male figure raising his right arm, with two smaller figures and serpents coiling around all three, highlighting the emotional intensity.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the depth of the composition, showing the figures' torsos and the serpents wrapping around their bodies from a profile perspective.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals the muscular back of the central figure and the arrangement of the other two figures, with the serpents' coils visible from behind.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the outstretched arm and the twisting forms of the figures, with the serpents' bodies clearly visible along the side.
attribute_subject_matter: Laocoön and his sons entangled by serpents
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a plinth, suggesting installation in a public or formal setting such as a plaza, park, or museum.
